title: Mariosousa centralis
intro: Mariosousa centralis, also known as the "Central American Mariosousa," is a species of tree within the Fabaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as the potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Mariosousa centralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat a range of ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Mariosousa centralis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a legume native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Mariosousa centralis is a member of the genus Mariosousa, which includes several other species of trees and shrubs. The exact classification of Mariosousa centralis is further refined to the family level as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. Within this subfamily, Mariosousa centralis is part of the tribe Sophoreae.
uses: Mariosousa centralis, also known as a tropical tree native to Central America, has been reported to have medicinal properties. Its bark is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The extracts from the plant have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Some research suggests that Mariosousa centralis may have applications in wound healing and as an antioxidant. properties: Mariosousa centralis is known for its medicinal properties, but limited research is available on its specific therapeutic uses. constituents: Mariosousa centralis contains various compounds including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol have been found in Mariosousa centralis, displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant also contains phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antitumor properties. Additionally, terpenoids present in Mariosousa centralis have been found to exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities.
